title: Night Hunt
desc: The desert comes alive after dark. So does what lives in it.

steps.text:
Night in the desert is a different world. The killing heat vanishes. The sand cools. And everything that spent the day hiding underground comes out to feed.

The hunting party assembles at dusk — six Mipedians carrying nets, spears, and bioluminescent lamps that cast a pale blue glow. The lead hunter briefs the group in whispers.

"Sand-runners tonight. They surface after dark to feed on dune beetles. Fast, skittish, and they burrow the instant they sense vibration. Move like ghosts. If I hear footsteps, you're out of the party."

Sand-runners are prized — their hide makes excellent leather, their meat is a delicacy, and their burrowing glands produce a chemical that Mipedian alchemists pay well for.

The lamps go out. The desert goes dark. Above, the stars are so thick they cast shadows.

options.label: Join the net team — surround and capture
Join the tracking team — find the burrow exits

steps.text:
The net team spreads wide, moving in a crescent across the sand. Your feet barely touch the ground — the Mipedian technique of sand-walking, distributing weight so evenly that the sand doesn't register your passage.

You hear them before you see them. A rapid skittering, like dry leaves blown across stone. The lead hunter raises a hand. Everyone freezes.

Three sand-runners emerge from a dune face — sleek, low to the ground, their scales reflecting starlight. They move in short bursts, pausing to probe the sand with long snouts, hunting beetles.

The crescent tightens. You move one step at a time, the net ready. The runners don't notice — they're focused on the ground, digging for prey.

The lead hunter clicks twice. The crescent collapses. Nets fly. Two runners are caught instantly, thrashing in silk mesh. The third bolts — a blur of motion across the starlit sand — and vanishes down a burrow before anyone reacts.

Two out of three. The hunter is satisfied. "Good catch. Clean, quiet, no injuries." The runners are carefully transferred to carrying cages, their legs bound with soft wraps to prevent burrowing.

You walk back under the stars, carrying a cage, feeling like the desert shared one of its secrets with you.

options.label: Return with the catch under the stars

steps.text:
The tracking team works ahead of the hunters, reading the sand for burrow exits. Sand-runners dig Y-shaped tunnels — one entrance, two exits — and they always run for the exit they didn't enter by.

You learn to spot the signs: a slight depression in the sand, a ring of tiny excavated grains, the faintest draft of warm air rising from below. Each exit is marked with a phosphorescent pin that glows green in the dark.

Within an hour, you've mapped eight burrow systems. The pins form a constellation on the dark sand — a map of invisible architecture.

The hunting team uses your map to position nets over the exits. When the runners surface to feed, they're driven back toward their burrows — right into the waiting nets.

Four runners caught, clean and unhurt. The lead hunter examines your burrow map and nods slowly. "You found two systems I've never seen before. That big one near the ridge — that's a matriarch burrow. Deep, complex, probably six runners living there."

She marks the matriarch burrow as protected — too valuable to harvest from. "We take from the singles. Leave the families. The desert provides if you don't take everything."

The hunting party returns before dawn, cages loaded, the stars fading into gray. You've learned to read the sand in the dark.

options.label: Return before dawn with new knowledge
